claims.claim_summary: Seasonal wheat crop water requirement (ETc) ranged ~155-220 mm across Punjab agro-climatic zones (CROPWAT).

claims.evidence_text: The total irrigation water requirement of wheat in MBD, FSD, TTS, BWN, BWP, and KNP is 44.7, 102.2, 103.1, 119.1, 179, and 184.2 mm, respectively.
claims.claim_summary: Wheat net irrigation water requirement ranged 44.7-184.2 mm across six Punjab districts, highest in dry south.

claims.evidence_text: the lowest water requirement is in the initial stage, and the highest water requirement is in the mid-stage during crop development ... the wheat crop requires more water during the growing stage than the other three stages
claims.claim_summary: Wheat water requirement is lowest at initial stage and highest at mid-season (development) stage.
